draft.introduction: 1 Timothy 6:11–21 contains the letter’s closing charge to Timothy, including the doxology declaring God as the one “who alone has immortality, who dwells in unapproachable light.” From Codex Sinaiticus.

translation.text: But as for you, O man of God, flee these things. Pursue righteousness, godliness, faith, love, steadfastness, gentleness. Fight the good fight of the faith. Take hold of the eternal life to which you were called.
translation.original_text: συ δε ω ανθρωπε θεου ταυτα φευγε

translation.text: Which he will display at the proper time—he who is the blessed and only Sovereign, the King of kings and Lord of lords, who alone has immortality, who dwells in unapproachable light, whom no one has ever seen or can see. To him be honor and eternal dominion. Amen.
translation.original_text: ο μακαριος και μονος δυναστης ο βασιλευς των βασιλευοντων

translator_notes.note: The phrase “unapproachable light” (φως οικων απρόσιτον) became central to the Palamite distinction between God’s essence (unknowable) and energies (participable). Gregory Palamas argued that theosis involves participation in the divine light without access to the divine essence.
